clean_output:
summary: The article keeps the main tension visible: labeled Claude Code pull requests were accepted less often than matched human pull requests, even though merged pull requests showed broadly similar revision and timing patterns.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: Agentic pull requests had a significantly lower acceptance rate than matched human pull requests, yet among pull requests that were merged, review timing and revision patterns were broadly similar.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Claude Code-generated pull requests had a significantly lower acceptance rate than matched human pull requests, at 83.77425% versus 91.00529%.
      - Among pull requests that were merged, Claude Code-generated pull requests moved through revision with outcomes broadly similar to matched human pull requests, including no significant difference in merge-without-revision rates and a median of two revised commits when revisions were needed.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-007
      - fact-008
      - fact-012
      - fact-013
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-07
      - evidence-08
      - evidence-12
      - evidence-13
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: The article must keep visible that the study shows an acceptance gap more clearly than it explains it; similar post-merge handling does not resolve why agentic pull requests were rejected more often upstream.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: The article discusses the acceptance gap as meaningful, but the rejection record does not reveal most rejection causes, leaving the apparent disadvantage only partially interpretable.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Claude Code-generated pull requests had a significantly lower acceptance rate than matched human pull requests, at 83.77425% versus 91.00529%.
      - Among pull requests that were merged, Claude Code-generated pull requests moved through revision with outcomes broadly similar to matched human pull requests, including no significant difference in merge-without-revision rates and a median of two revised commits when revisions were needed.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-009
      - fact-010
      - fact-011
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-09
      - evidence-10
      - evidence-11
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: It must stay visible that some non-merges were procedural rather than code-quality failures, and that most rejected agentic pull requests were closed without explanatory discussion, so the mechanism behind the acceptance gap remains unobserved.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: The acceptance comparison is presented as a comparison of agentic versus human pull requests, but the sampled agentic set is restricted to explicitly labeled Claude Code pull requests and excludes many unmatched cases, which limits how directly the gap can be generalized.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Claude Code-generated pull requests had a significantly lower acceptance rate than matched human pull requests, at 83.77425% versus 91.00529%.
      - The study's agentic PR dataset is bounded to pull requests explicitly labeled "Generated with Claude Code" in GitHub descriptions, drawn from repositories with at least 10 stars between February 24, 2025 and April 30, 2025, with additional exclusions during comparison-set construction.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-002
      - fact-003
      - fact-005
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-02
      - evidence-03
      - evidence-05
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: The article should keep clear that the reported gap applies to labeled, filtered, and matchable Claude Code pull requests, not to all agent-assisted coding on GitHub.
